name: model-scope
description: Turn ANY parameterised model into an interactive explorer: move a slider per parameter and watch the simulation update, shown in views the MODEL defines — no fixed graphic or axis. Philosophy: don't reproduce a paper's figure — decompose the model's process to the ATOMIC level and show input → transformation → output at THREE zoom levels via a level switch (lenses): ⚛ Step (one update split into signal/leak/noise → new state), ◷ Trial (the atom repeated over time → an output), ∑ Simulation (many trials → the statistics). A Bayesian observer draws prior/likelihood/posterior; a neuron draws a trace + raster + f–I curve; a decision model decomposes one accumulation step, one trial, then the RT histogram. Animation idioms: a continuous playhead, a process-mode STAGE stepper, and the three-level lens switch. Build a whole PAPER as a few screens (mechanism → condition comparisons → prediction), separating experimental conditions from model parameters and making the claim↔mechanism mapping visible; heavy screens stream via runChunks with a loading overlay. Schema-driven sliders + a simulate()→views contract + a small plotting helper, no build step. Includes a modelbook of canonical families (Bayesian/efficient-coding observers, causal inference, working memory, decision circuits, neurons, RL, psychophysics) grounded in real open source, a reusable code library, and a GUI QC pipeline (static gate + visual checklist + two-axis review).
